messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "ce7-hyperscale-mix-mirage",
 "title": "The 0.91 rescale imports hyperscale that isn't in CE-7: the segment mix is misallocated even though the TAM headline doesn't move",
 "narrative": "seg.hyperscale applies Synergy's global 48% hyperscale capacity share to Central Europe via a flat 0.91 carve-out. But ~60% of Europe's operational hyperscale self-build capacity sits in Ireland, the Netherlands, Sweden and Belgium — three of the four outside CE-7 — and Europe's self-build growth (24% YoY) keeps landing disproportionately in those markets. CE-7 is structurally colo- and enterprise-weighted relative to the global mix the model borrowed. Because segment shares sum to the same base, the TAM headline is untouched and no alarm rings; but the SAM haircut logic, certification priorities and Year-1 pipeline are all built on a buyer mix that overweights hyperscale by perhaps a third. This is a resourcing kill, not a sizing kill.",
 "category": "model-structure",
 "targetNodes": [
  "seg.hyperscale",
  "seg.colocation",
  "seg.enterprise"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Synergy's global 48% hyperscale share is transplanted to CE-7 through a flat 0.91 carve-out, but Europe's hyperscale self-build capacity is concentrated (~60%) in Ireland, NL, Sweden and Belgium — a geographic pattern that makes the global split a poor proxy for CE-7",
   "refs": [
    "digest:seg-mix#c0",
    "digest:seg-mix#c14"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "European hyperscale self-build grows 24% YoY versus colo's 19%, and that growth keeps concentrating outside CE-7, so the divergence between the borrowed global mix and the true CE-7 mix widens through exactly the venture's entry window rather than converging",
   "refs": [
    "digest:seg-mix#c12",
    "digest:seg-mix#c13"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The €300M base is misallocated across segments: hyperscale overweighted, colo/enterprise underweighted. Since shares sum to the same TAM, the headline never flags the error — but which buyers to certify for, which channels to build and where Year-1 pipeline effort goes are all keyed to the wrong weights",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:seg.hyperscale",
    "ledger:seg.colocation",
    "ledger:seg.enterprise"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "A wrong segment mix leaves TAM, SAM and YAM totals identical, so every number the client's team has reviewed reads the same; the tornado tests each share's band in isolation but never tests whether the mix as a whole was transplanted from the wrong geography. The 0.91 carve-out looks like a careful adjustment, which disguises that the underlying split was never Europe-scoped.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "A Europe- or CE-scoped capacity split (CBRE, Synergy regional cut, or JLL) showing hyperscale-owned/operated capacity at ≥40% of CE-7 MW, validating the transplanted global share.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.45,
  "rationale": "The 60%-outside-CE concentration figure is directly sourced and the ledger's own promotion path admits no Europe-scoped split exists; the main uncertainty is how much hyperscale leased-in-colo capacity within CE offsets the self-build concentration elsewhere",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "seg.hyperscale",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.3,
   "note": "CE-7 hyperscale-owned share corrected for self-build concentration outside CE"
  },
  {
   "nodeId": "seg.colocation",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.26,
   "note": "Mix-shift: CE capacity is more colo-weighted than the global split implies"
  },
  {
   "nodeId": "seg.enterprise",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.33,
   "note": "Mix-shift: DACH enterprise/self-operated weight (BMWK, Swiss self-operation) raises the enterprise share; siblings moved explicitly since the engine does not re-normalize"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "First publication of a Europe or CE regional capacity split by operator type",
   "where": "Synergy Research regional releases; CBRE European Data Centres quarterly reports",
   "threshold": "CE/Europe hyperscale-owned share reported ≥8pp below the model's 0.44",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Location of announced hyperscale self-build campuses in Europe",
   "where": "DCD/DCNN build announcements; German and Polish planning/permit registries for hyperscale campuses",
   "threshold": "≥70% of announced European self-build MW landing outside CE-7 for two consecutive quarters",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Purchase or commission a CE-7-scoped capacity split by operator type (hyperscale-owned vs colo vs enterprise) before locking Year-1 channel investment",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "seg.hyperscale",
   "note": "Replaces the transplanted global split — the single highest-leverage structural fix in the segment dimension"
  },
  {
   "action": "Weight Year-1 pipeline and certification spend toward colo and enterprise accounts in DE/NL rather than hyperscale framework pursuit",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Hedges the mix error: these buyers exist in CE at scale under either mix scenario"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "boundary-substitution",
 "riskId": "risk.boundary-substitution.ce7-hyperscale-mix-mirage"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

German and Polish planning/permit registries for hyperscale campuses","threshold":"≥70% of announced European self-build MW landing outside CE-7 for two consecutive quarters","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Purchase or commission a CE-7-scoped capacity split by operator type (hyperscale-owned vs colo vs enterprise) before locking Year-1 channel investment","type":"information","voiNodeId":"seg.hyperscale","note":"Replaces the transplanted global split — the single highest-leverage structural fix in the segment dimension"},{"action":"Weight Year-1 pipeline and certification spend toward colo and enterprise accounts in DE/NL rather than hyperscale framework pursuit","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Hedges the mix error: these buyers exist in CE at scale under either mix scenario"}]}
